designation: Principal Software Engineer, Creator of setup-php

projects.name: setup-php
projects.description: Setup PHP with required extensions, php.ini configuration, code-coverage support and various tools like composer in GitHub Actions. This action gives you a cross-platform interface to set up the PHP environment you need to test your application.
projects.short_description: GitHub action to set up PHP with extensions, php.ini configuration, coverage drivers, and various tools.

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response: GitHub action to set up PHP with extensions, php.ini configuration, coverage drivers, and various tools.

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
form.response: “Ship early, ship often”: I do not like to wait for covering every edge case before publishing, rather I would invite real users in, learn from their feedback, and iterate.

form.question: Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
form.response: Every thank-you comment, PR, or bug report reminds me that open source is a team sport—and that sense of community keeps me going.

form.question: If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
form.response: Its is a bit dramatic, but Eminem - Lose Yourself

form.question: Which file in your project would you most like to set on fire?
form.response: https://github.com/shivammathur/setup-php/blob/main/src/configs/tools.json. I would like to build a proper registry where projects can register their tools to add support in a standard way.

form.question: What's your open-source villain origin story?
form.response: It began with a burnout at a devops role implementing the same thing across different projects and it taking a long time due to how broken the tooling was before setup-php to implement PHP CI.

form.question: If you had to use one emoji to convey what it is like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
